channel.title: Kissimmee Planning Advisory Board - Public Notices
channel.description: Public notices for Kissimmee Planning Advisory Board meetings and proceedings

item.title: Miscellaneous Notices - Kissimmee
item.description:
NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING ORDINANCE NO
--- Full Notice Text --- NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING ORDINANCE NO. 2026-12
Notice is hereby given that the Planning Commission of the City of St. Cloud, Florida, at a meeting to be held on March 17, 2026, in the Council Chambers at City Hall, 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, Florida at 6:00 p.m., will consider the issue and make a recommendation to the City Council relative to enactment of Ordinance No. 2026-12 entitled:
Notice is hereby given that the City Council of the City of St. Cloud, Florida, at a meeting to be held on April 9, 2026, in their Chamber at City Hall, 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, Florida at 6:30 p.m., will consider the adoption of Ordinance No. 2026-12 entitled:
AN ORDINANCE OF THE CITY COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF ST. CLOUD, FLORIDA, PROVIDING FOR THE ZONING AMENDMENT OF THE ZONING MAP OF THE LAND DEVELOPMENT CODE OF THE CITY OF ST. CLOUD, FLORIDA, SPECIFICALLY CHANGING THE ZONING DESIGNATION OF APPROXIMATELY +/- 9.79 ACRES IDENTIFIED AS NARCOOSSEE AND STARLINE, PROJECT #ZMA26-00001, FROM "AC" AGRICULTURE AND CONSERVATION AND R-2 RURAL DEVELOPMENT (COUNTY) TO "HB" HIGHWAY BUSINESS (CITY), GENERALLY LOCATED NORTH OF J STREET, EAST OF TWISTED BRANCH LANE, SOUTH OF LILLIAN BLACK ROAD, AND WEST OF SOUTH NARCOOSSEE ROAD; PROVIDING FOR ENTERING THE DESIGNATION ON THE OFFICIAL ZONING MAP, FILING OF THE PLANNING COMMISSION RECOMMENDATION AND PROOF OF PUBLICATION, SEVERABILITY, AND EFFECTIVE DATE.
Interested parties may appear at the meeting at which time this ORDINANCE is to be considered and shall be heard with respect to the proposed ORDINANCE at the Public Hearing thereon. This ORDINANCE, in its entirety can be reviewed at the Community Development Department between the hours of 8:00 a.m. and 4:00 p.m. Monday through Friday.
If a person decides to appeal any decision made by the Committee/Board, with respect to any matter considered at such hearing/meeting, such person will need a record of the proceedings and that, for this purpose, such person may need to ensure that a verbatim record of the proceedings is made, which record includes the testimony and evidence upon which the appeal is to be based, and which record is not provided by the City of St. Cloud. (FS 286.0105)
In accordance with the Americans with Disabilities Act, persons needing assistance to participate in any of these proceedings should contact the Secretary/Clerk of the Committee/Board (listed below), prior to the meeting. (FS 286.26)
Christy Depuy, Planning Commission Secretary 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, FL 34769 Telephone: (407) 957-7297
Ivy Llauro, City Clerk 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, Florida 34769 Telephone: 407-957-7300 March 5, 2026

item.title: Miscellaneous Notices - Kissimmee
item.description:
NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING ORDINANCE NO
--- Full Notice Text --- NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING ORDINANCE NO. 2026-14
Notice is hereby given that the Planning Commission of the City of St. Cloud, Florida, at a meeting to be held on March 17, 2026, in the Council Chambers at City Hall, 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, Florida at 6:00 p.m., will consider the issue and make a recommendation to the City Council relative to enactment of Ordinance No. 2026-14 entitled:
Notice is hereby given that the City Council of the City of St. Cloud, Florida, at a meeting to be held on April 9, 2026, in their Chamber at City Hall, 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, Florida at 6:30 p.m., will consider the adoption of Ordinance No. 2026-14 entitled:
AN ORDINANCE OF THE CITY COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF ST. CLOUD, FLORIDA, ASSIGNING A FUTURE LAND USE DESIGNATION OF APPROXIMATELY +/- 4.85 ACRES IDENTIFIED AS HICKORY TREE ROAD COMMUNITY CENTER, PROJECT #CPA25-00016, GENERALLY LOCATED NORTH OF OLIVE MOUNTAIN BEND, EAST OF OLD HICKORY TREE ROAD, SOUTH OF NOLTE ROAD, AND WEST OF HICKORY TREE ROAD; FROM CC COMMUNITY CENTER (COUNTY) TO CC COMMUNITY CENTER (CITY), PROVIDING FOR AMENDING THE OFFICIAL FUTURE LAND USE MAP OF THE COMPREHENSIVE PLAN, FILING OF THE PLANNING COMMISSION RECOMMENDATION AND PROOF OF PUBLICATION, APPLICABILITY AND EFFECT, SEVERABILITY, COPIES ON FILE AND EFFECTIVE DATE.
Interested parties may appear at the meeting at which time this ORDINANCE is to be considered and shall be heard with respect to the proposed ORDINANCE at the Public Hearing thereon. This ORDINANCE, in its entirety can be reviewed at the Community Development Department between the hours of 8:00 a.m. and 4:00 p.m. Monday through Friday.
If a person decides to appeal any decision made by the Committee/Board, with respect to any matter considered at such hearing/meeting, such person will need a record of the proceedings and that, for this purpose, such person may need to ensure that a verbatim record of the proceedings is made, which record includes the testimony and evidence upon which the appeal is to be based, and which record is not provided by the City of St. Cloud. (FS 286.0105)
In accordance with the Americans with Disabilities Act, persons needing assistance to participate in any of these proceedings should contact the Secretary/Clerk of the Committee/Board (listed below), prior to the meeting. (FS 286.26)
Christy Depuy, Planning Commission Secretary 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, FL 34769 Telephone: (407) 957-7297
Ivy Llauro, City Clerk 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, Florida 34769 Telephone: 407-957-7300 March 5, 2026

item.title: Miscellaneous Notices - Kissimmee
item.description:
NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING ORDINANCE NO
--- Full Notice Text --- NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING ORDINANCE NO. 2026-07
Notice is hereby given that the Planning Commission of the City of St. Cloud, Florida, at a meeting to be held on March 17, 2026, in the Council Chambers at City Hall, 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, Florida at 6:00 p.m., will consider the issue and make a recommendation to the City Council relative to enactment of Ordinance No. 2026-07 entitled:
Notice is hereby given that the City Council of the City of St. Cloud, Florida, at a meeting to be held on April 9, 2026, in their Chamber at City Hall, 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, Florida at 6:30 p.m., will consider the adoption of Ordinance No. 2026-07 entitled:
AN ORDINANCE OF THE CITY COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF ST. CLOUD, FLORIDA, ASSIGNING A FUTURE LAND USE DESIGNATION OF APPROXIMATELY +/- 743.53 ACRES IDENTIFIED AS WHALEY PLATT PROJECT #CPA25-00011, GENERALLY LOCATED NORTH OF LAKE TOHOPEKALIGA, EAST OF OSCEOLA ROAD, SOUTH OF KISSIMMEE PARK ROAD, AND WEST OF LAKE TOHOPEKALIGA ROAD; FROM MXD MIXED USE (COUNTY) TO MIX MIXED USE (CITY), PROVIDING FOR AMENDING THE OFFICIAL FUTURE LAND USE MAP OF THE COMPREHENSIVE PLAN, FILING OF THE PLANNING COMMISSION RECOMMENDATION AND PROOF OF PUBLICATION, APPLICABILITY AND EFFECT, SEVERABILITY, COPIES ON FILE AND EFFECTIVE DATE.
Interested parties may appear at the meeting at which time this ORDINANCE is to be considered and shall be heard with respect to the proposed ORDINANCE at the Public Hearing thereon. This ORDINANCE, in its entirety can be reviewed at the Community Development Department between the hours of 8:00 a.m. and 4:00 p.m. Monday through Friday.
If a person decides to appeal any decision made by the Committee/Board, with respect to any matter considered at such hearing/meeting, such person will need a record of the proceedings and that, for this purpose, such person may need to ensure that a verbatim record of the proceedings is made, which record includes the testimony and evidence upon which the appeal is to be based, and which record is not provided by the City of St. Cloud. (FS 286.0105)
In accordance with the Americans with Disabilities Act, persons needing assistance to participate in any of these proceedings should contact the Secretary/Clerk of the Committee/Board (listed below), prior to the meeting. (FS 286.26)
Christy Depuy, Planning Commission Secretary 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, FL 34769 Telephone: (407) 957-7297
Ivy Llauro, City Clerk 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, Florida 34769 Telephone: 407-957-7300 March 5, 2026

item.title: Miscellaneous Notices - Kissimmee
item.description:
NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING ORDINANCE NO
--- Full Notice Text --- NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING ORDINANCE NO. 2026-15
Notice is hereby given that the Planning Commission of the City of St. Cloud, Florida, at a meeting to be held on March 17, 2026, in the Council Chambers at City Hall, 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, Florida at 6:00 p.m., will consider the issue and make a recommendation to the City Council relative to enactment of Ordinance No. 2026-15 entitled:
Notice is hereby given that the City Council of the City of St. Cloud, Florida, at a meeting to be held on April 9, 2026, in their Chamber at City Hall, 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, Florida at 6:30 p.m., will consider the adoption of Ordinance No. 2026-15 entitled:
AN ORDINANCE OF THE CITY COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF ST. CLOUD, FLORIDA, PROVIDING FOR THE ZONING AMENDMENT OF THE ZONING MAP OF THE LAND DEVELOPMENT CODE OF THE CITY OF ST. CLOUD, FLORIDA, SPECIFICALLY CHANGING THE ZONING DESIGNATION OF APPROXIMATELY +/- 4.85 ACRES IDENTIFIED AS HICKORY TREE ROAD COMMUNITY CENTER, PROJECT #ZMA25-00020, FROM "CCC" COMMUNITY CENTER CORE (COUNTY) TO "CCC" COMMUNITY CENTER CORE (CITY), GENERALLY LOCATED NORTH OF OLIVE MOUNTAIN BEND, EAST OF OLD HICKORY TREE ROAD, SOUTH OF NOLTE ROAD, AND WEST OF HICKORY TREE ROAD; PROVIDING FOR ENTERING THE DESIGNATION ON THE OFFICIAL ZONING MAP, FILING OF THE PLANNING COMMISSION RECOMMENDATION AND PROOF OF PUBLICATION, SEVERABILITY, AND EFFECTIVE DATE.
Interested parties may appear at the meeting at which time this ORDINANCE is to be considered and shall be heard with respect to the proposed ORDINANCE at the Public Hearing thereon. This ORDINANCE, in its entirety can be reviewed at the Community Development Department between the hours of 8:00 a.m. and 4:00 p.m. Monday through Friday.
If a person decides to appeal any decision made by the Committee/Board, with respect to any matter considered at such hearing/meeting, such person will need a record of the proceedings and that, for this purpose, such person may need to ensure that a verbatim record of the proceedings is made, which record includes the testimony and evidence upon which the appeal is to be based, and which record is not provided by the City of St. Cloud. (FS 286.0105)
In accordance with the Americans with Disabilities Act, persons needing assistance to participate in any of these proceedings should contact the Secretary/Clerk of the Committee/Board (listed below), prior to the meeting. (FS 286.26)
Christy Depuy, Planning Commission Secretary 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, FL 34769 Telephone: (407) 957-7297
Ivy Llauro, City Clerk 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, Florida 34769 Telephone: 407-957-7300 March 5, 2026

item.title: Miscellaneous Notices - Kissimmee
item.description:
Notice at 1215 OLD HICKORY TREE ROAD
Location: 1215 OLD HICKORY TREE ROAD
--- Full Notice Text --- NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING RESOLUTION NO. 2026-023R
Notice is hereby given that the Planning Commission of the City of St. Cloud, Florida, at a meeting to be held on March 17, 2026, in the Council Chambers at City Hall, 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, Florida at 6:00 p.m., will consider the issue and make a recommendation to the City Council relative to enactment of Resolution No. 2026-023R entitled:
Notice is hereby given that the City Council of the City of St. Cloud, Florida, at a meeting to be held on April 9, 2026, in the Council Chambers at City Hall, 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, Florida at 6:30 p.m., will consider the adoption of Resolution No. 2026-023R entitled:
A RESOLUTION OF THE CITY COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF ST. CLOUD, FLORIDA, GRANTING A CONDITIONAL USE FOR A PRIVATE SCHOOL IN THE SINGLE-FAMILY DWELLING R-1B ZONING DISTRICT ON THE +/- 2.45 ACRES OF LAND KNOWN AS GRACEFUL CHRISTIAN ACADEMY, PROJECT #CNU25-00008, LOCATED AT 1215 OLD HICKORY TREE ROAD, AS DESCRIBED IN THE BODY OF THIS RESOLUTION..
Interested parties may appear at the meeting at which time this RESOLUTION is to be considered and shall be heard with respect to the proposed RESOLUTION at the Public Hearing thereon. This RESOLUTION, in its entirety, can be reviewed at the Community Development Department between the hours of 8:00 a.m. and 4:00 p.m. Monday through Friday.
If a person decides to appeal any decision made by the Committee/Board, with respect to any matter considered at such hearing/meeting, such person will need a record of the proceedings and that, for this purpose, such person may need to ensure that a verbatim record of the proceedings is made, which record includes the testimony and evidence upon which the appeal is to be based, and which record is not provided by the City of St. Cloud. (FS 286.0105)
In accordance with the Americans with Disabilities Act, persons needing assistance to participate in any of these proceedings should contact the Secretary/Clerk of the Committee/Board (listed below), prior to the meeting. (FS 286.26)
Christy Depuy, Planning Commission Secretary 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, FL 34769 Telephone: (407) 957-7297
Ivy Llauro, City Clerk 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, Florida 34769 Telephone: 407-957-7300 March 5, 2026

item.title: Miscellaneous Notices - Kissimmee
item.description:
NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING ORDINANCE NO
--- Full Notice Text --- NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING ORDINANCE NO. 2026-09
Notice is hereby given that the Planning Commission of the City of St. Cloud, Florida, at a meeting to be held on March 17, 2026, in the Council Chambers at City Hall, 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, Florida at 6:00 p.m., will consider the issue and make a recommendation to the City Council relative to enactment of Ordinance No. 2026-09 entitled:
Notice is hereby given that the City Council of the City of St. Cloud, Florida, at a meeting to be held on April 9, 2026, in their Chamber at City Hall, 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, Florida at 6:30 p.m., will consider the adoption of Ordinance No. 2026-09 entitled:
AN ORDINANCE OF THE CITY COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF ST. CLOUD, FLORIDA, AMENDING ARTICLE 13 SPECIAL PURPOSE OVERLAY DISTRICTS, SECTION 3.13.3, ENTERTAINMENT DISTRICT, AMENDING SUBSECTION C OUTSIDE CONSUMPTION IN THE DISTRICT SUBPARAGRAPH 1 DISTRICT POSSESSION/CONSUMPTION HOURS REVISING THE DAYS AND HOURS FOR THE AUTHORIZED CONSUMPTION OF ALCOHOLIC BEVERAGES WITHIN THE ENTERTAINMENT DISTRICT; PROVIDING FOR CONFLICT, SEVERABILITY, ADMINISTRATIVE CORRECTION, CODIFICATION AND PUBLICATION AND EFFECTIVE DATE.
Interested parties may appear at the meeting at which time this ORDINANCE is to be considered and shall be heard with respect to the proposed ORDINANCE at the Public Hearing thereon. This ORDINANCE, in its entirety can be reviewed at the Community Development Department between the hours of 8:00 a.m. and 4:00 p.m. Monday through Friday.
If a person decides to appeal any decision made by the Committee/Board, with respect to any matter considered at such hearing/meeting, such person will need a record of the proceedings and that, for this purpose, such person may need to ensure that a verbatim record of the proceedings is made, which record includes the testimony and evidence upon which the appeal is to be based, and which record is not provided by the City of St. Cloud. (FS 286.0105)
In accordance with the Americans with Disabilities Act, persons needing assistance to participate in any of these proceedings should contact the Secretary/Clerk of the Committee/Board (listed below), prior to the meeting. (FS 286.26)
Christy Depuy, Planning Commission Secretary 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, FL 34769 Telephone: (407) 957-7297
Ivy Llauro, City Clerk 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, Florida 34769 Telephone: 407-957-7300 March 5, 2026

item.title: Miscellaneous Notices - Kissimmee
item.description:
NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING ORDINANCE NO
--- Full Notice Text --- NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING ORDINANCE NO. 2026-11
Notice is hereby given that the Planning Commission of the City of St. Cloud, Florida, at a meeting to be held on March 17, 2026, in the Council Chambers at City Hall, 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, Florida at 6:00 p.m., will consider the issue and make a recommendation to the City Council relative to enactment of Ordinance No. 2026-11 entitled:
Notice is hereby given that the City Council of the City of St. Cloud, Florida, at ameeting to be held on April 9, 2026, in their Chamber at City Hall, 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, Florida at 6:30 p.m., will consider the adoption of Ordinance No. 2026-11 entitled:
AN ORDINANCE OF THE CITY COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF ST. CLOUD, FLORIDA, ASSIGNING A FUTURE LAND USE DESIGNATION OF APPROXIMATELY +/- 9.79 ACRES IDENTIFIED AS NARCOOSSEE AND STARLINE, PROJECT #CPA26-00001, GENERALLY LOCATED NORTH OF J STREET, EAST OF TWISTED BRANCH LANE, SOUTH OF LILLIAN BLACK ROAD, AND WEST OF SOUTH NARCOOSSEE ROAD; FROM LDR LOW DENSITY RESIDENTIAL (COUNTY) TO COM COMMERCIAL (CITY), PROVIDING FOR AMENDING THE OFFICIAL FUTURE LAND USE MAP OF THE COMPREHENSIVE PLAN, FILING OF THE PLANNING COMMISSION RECOMMENDATION AND PROOF OF PUBLICATION, APPLICABILITY AND EFFECT, SEVERABILITY, COPIES ON FILE AND EFFECTIVE DATE.
Interested parties may appear at the meeting at which time this ORDINANCE is to be considered and shall be heard with respect to the proposed ORDINANCE at the Public Hearing thereon. This ORDINANCE, in its entirety can be reviewed at the Community Development Department between the hours of 8:00 a.m. and 4:00 p.m. Monday through Friday.
If a person decides to appeal any decision made by the Committee/Board, with respect to any matter considered at such hearing/meeting, such person will need a record of the proceedings and that, for this purpose, such person may need to ensure that a verbatim record of the proceedings is made, which record includes the testimony and evidence upon which the appeal is to be based, and which record is not provided by the City of St. Cloud. (FS 286.0105)
In accordance with the Americans with Disabilities Act, persons needing assistance to participate in any of these proceedings should contact the Secretary/Clerk of the Committee/Board (listed below), prior to the meeting. (FS 286.26)
Christy Depuy, Planning Commission Secretary 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, FL 34769 Telephone: Telephone: (407) 957-7297
Ivy Llauro, City Clerk 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, Florida 34769 Telephone: 407-957-7300 March 5, 2026

item.title: Miscellaneous Notices - Kissimmee
item.description:
NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING ORDINANCE NO
--- Full Notice Text --- NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING ORDINANCE NO. 2026-17
Notice is hereby given that the Planning Commission of the City of St. Cloud, Florida, at a meeting to be held on March 17, 2026, in the Council Chambers at City Hall, 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, Florida at 6:00 p.m., will consider the issue and make a recommendation to the City Council relative to enactment of Ordinance No. 2026-17 entitled:
Notice is hereby given that the City Council of the City of St. Cloud, Florida, at a meeting to be held on April 9, 2026, in their Chamber at City Hall, 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, Florida at 6:30 p.m., will consider the adoption of Ordinance No. 2026-17 entitled:
AN ORDINANCE OF THE CITY COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF ST. CLOUD, FLORIDA, AMENDING CHAPTER 3, ARTICLE 4, AGRICULTURAL ZONING DISTRICTS; ARTICLE 5, RESIDENTIAL ZONING DISTRICTS; AND ARTICLE 14, USE REGULATIONS, SECTION 3.14.30 ACCESSORY DWELLING UNITS (ADU) OF THE LAND DEVELOPMENT CODE. PROVIDING FOR SEVERABILITY, CONFLICTS, CODIFICATION, PUBLICATION AND AN EFFECTIVE DATE.
Interested parties may appear at the meeting at which time this ORDINANCE is to be considered and shall be heard with respect to the proposed ORDINANCE at the Public Hearing thereon. This ORDINANCE, in its entirety can be reviewed at the Community Development Department between the hours of 8:00 a.m. and 4:00 p.m. Monday through Friday.
If a person decides to appeal any decision made by the Committee/Board, with respect to any matter considered at such hearing/meeting, such person will need a record of the proceedings and that, for this purpose, such person may need to ensure that a verbatim record of the proceedings is made, which record includes the testimony and evidence upon which the appeal is to be based, and which record is not provided by the City of St. Cloud. (FS 286.0105)
In accordance with the Americans with Disabilities Act, persons needing assistance to participate in any of these proceedings should contact the Secretary/Clerk of the Committee/Board (listed below), prior to the meeting. (FS 286.26)
Christy Depuy, Planning Commission Secretary 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, FL 34769 Telephone: (407) 957-7297
Ivy Llauro, City Clerk 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, Florida 34769 Telephone: 407-957-7300 March 5, 2026

item.title: Miscellaneous Notices - Kissimmee
item.description:
Notice at 8 Broadway
Location: 8 Broadway
--- Full Notice Text --- EDGEWATER EAST COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT DISTRICT NOTICE OF MEETING
Notice is hereby given that the Board of Supervisors (Board) of the Edgewater East Community Development District (District) will hold a Regular Meeting on March 20, 2026, at 9:00 a.m., at the offices of Hanson, Walter & Associates, Inc., located at 8 Broadway, Suite 104, Kissimmee, Florida 34741.
The purpose of the meeting is for the Board to consider any business that may be lawfully considered. The meeting is open to the public and will be conducted in accordance with the provision of Florida Law for Community Development Districts. The meeting may be continued to a date, time, and place to be specified on the record at the meeting. A copy of the agenda for the meeting may be obtained from the office of the District Manager, Wrathell, Hunt & Associates, LLC, 2300 Glades Road, Suite 410W, Boca Raton, Florida 33431, (877) 276-0889, during normal business hours or by visiting the districts website, http://edgewatereastcdd.net/.
There may be occasions when one or more Supervisors or staff will participate by telephone. Pursuant to provisions of the Americans with Disabilities Act, any person requiring special accommodations at this meeting because of a disability or physical impairment should contact the District Office at (877) 276-0889 at least 48 hours prior to the meeting. If you are hearing or speech impaired, please contact the Florida Relay Service by dialing 7-1-1, or 1-800-955-8771 (TTY) / 1-800-955-8770 (Voice), for aid in contacting the District Office.
A person who decides to appeal any decision made at the meeting with respect to any matter considered at the meeting is advised that person will need a record of the proceedings and that accordingly, the person may need to ensure that a verbatim record of the proceedings is made, including the testimony and evidence upon which such appeal is to be based.
District Manager February 26, 2026

item.title: Miscellaneous Notices - Kissimmee
item.description:
SOUTH LAKE TOHO COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT DISTRICT NOTICE OF MEETING
The Board of Supervisors (Board) of the South Lake Toho Community Development Di...
--- Full Notice Text --- SOUTH LAKE TOHO COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT DISTRICT NOTICE OF MEETING
The Board of Supervisors (Board) of the South Lake Toho Community Development District (District) will hold a regular meeting on March 5, 2026, at 10:00 a.m., at the Hart Memorial Library, 211 East Dakin Avenue, First Floor, Room 120, Kissimmee, Florida 34741.
The purpose of the meeting is for the Board to consider any business which may properly come before it. The meeting is open to the public and will be conducted in accordance with the provision of Florida law. The meeting may be continued to a date, time, and place to be specified on the record at the meeting. A copy of the agenda for the meeting may be obtained by contacting the offices of the District Manager, Wrathell, Hunt & Associates, LLC, 2300 Glades Road, Suite 410W, Boca Raton, Florida 33431, (561) 571-0010, during normal business hours.
There may be occasions when one or more Supervisors or staff will participate by telephone. Pursuant to provisions of the Americans with Disabilities Act, any person requiring special accommodations at the meeting because of a disability or physical impairment should contact the District Office at (561) 571-0010 at least 48 hours prior to the meeting. If you are hearing or speech impaired, please contact the Florida Relay Service by dialing 7-1-1, or 1-800-955-8771 (TTY) / 1-800-955-8770 (Voice), for aid in contacting the District Office.
A person who decides to appeal any decision made at the meeting with respect to any matter considered at the meeting is advised that person will need a record of the proceedings and that accordingly, the person may need to ensure that a verbatim record of the proceedings is made, including the testimony and evidence upon which such appeal is to be based.
District Manager February 26, 2026

item.title: Miscellaneous Notices - Kissimmee
item.description:
Notice at 8 Broadway
Location: 8 Broadway
--- Full Notice Text --- EDGEWATER WEST COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT DISTRICT NOTICE OF MEETINGS
Notice is hereby given that the Board of Supervisors (Board) of the Edgewater West Community Development District (District), will hold a Regular Meeting on March 5, 2026, at 9:15 a.m., or as soon thereafter as the matter may be heard, at the offices of Hanson, Walter & Associates, Inc., 8 Broadway, Suite 104, Kissimmee, Florida 34741 for the purpose of considering any business that may be lawfully considered.
The Board will hold a Special Meeting on March 20, 2026, at 9:15 a.m., or as soon thereafter as the matter may be heard, at the offices of Hanson, Walter & Associates, Inc., located at 8 Broadway, Suite 104, Kissimmee, Florida 34741 for the purpose of adopting a supplemental assessment resolution with delegated authority as well as consider any other business that may be lawfully considered.
The meetings are open to the public and will be conducted in accordance with the provision of Florida Law for Community Development Districts. The meetings may be continued to a date, time, and place to be specified on the record at the meetings. A copy of the agenda for the meetings may be obtained from the office of the District Manager, Wrathell, Hunt & Associates, LLC, 2300 Glades Road, Suite 410W, Boca Raton, Florida 33431, (877) 276-0889, during normal business hours or by visiting the districts website, https://edgewaterwestcdd.net/.
There may be occasions when one or more Supervisors or staff will participate by telephone. Pursuant to provisions of the Americans with Disabilities Act, any person requiring special accommodations at the meetings because of a disability or physical impairment should contact the District Office at (877) 276-0889 at least 48 hours prior to the meetings. If you are hearing or speech impaired, please contact the Florida Relay Service by dialing 7-1-1, or 1-800-955-8771 (TTY) / 1-800-955-8770 (Voice), for aid in contacting the District Office.
A person who decides to appeal any decision made at the meetings with respect to any matter considered at the meetings is advised that person will need a record of the proceedings and that accordingly, the person may need to ensure that a verbatim record of the proceedings is made, including the testimony and evidence upon which such appeal is to be based.
District Manager February 26, 2026

item.title: Miscellaneous Notices - Kissimmee
item.description:
Notice at 951 Martin Luther King Boulevard
Location: 951 Martin Luther King Boulevard
--- Full Notice Text --- THE WATER COOPERATIVE OF CENTRAL FLORIDA NOTICE OF CANCELLATION OF BOARD MEETING SCHEDULED FOR MARCH 4, 2026 The Water Cooperative of Central Florida Board of Supervisors meeting scheduled for March 4, 2026, has been cancelled. The next meeting of the Board of Supervisors is scheduled for April 1, 2026, at 2:00 PM and will be held in the Bruce R. Van Meter Board Room at the Toho Water Authority administration building located at 951 Martin Luther King Boulevard, Kissimmee, FL 34741. Contact Administration Office (t: 407-944-5130 or administrativeoffice@tohowater.com for further assistance. February 26, 2026

item.title: Miscellaneous Notices - Kissimmee
item.description:
NOTICE OF RULEMAKING REGARDING THE RULES OF PROCEDURE OF THE SOUTH LAKE TOHO COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT DISTRICT
In accordance with Chapters 120 and 1...
--- Full Notice Text --- NOTICE OF RULEMAKING REGARDING THE RULES OF PROCEDURE OF THE SOUTH LAKE TOHO COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT DISTRICT
In accordance with Chapters 120 and 190, Florida Statutes, the District hereby gives the public notice of its intent to adopt its proposed Rules of Procedure (the Proposed Rule). The Proposed Rule number is 2026-1. Prior notice of rule development relative to the Proposed Rule was published in the Osceola News Gazette on February 19, 2026.
A public hearing will be conducted by the Board of Supervisors (the Board) of the South Lake Toho Community Development District (the District) on April 2, 2026, at 10:00 a.m. at the Hart Memorial Library, 211 East Dakin Avenue, First Floor, Room 120 - HMC, Kissimmee, Florida 34741, relative to the adoption of the Proposed Rule. Pursuant to Sections 190.011(5) and 190.012(3), Florida Statutes, the Proposed Rule will not require legislative ratification.
The purpose and effect of the Proposed Rule is to provide for efficient and effective District operations and ensure compliance with recent changes to Florida Law. The specific grant of rulemaking authority for the adoption of the Proposed Rule includes Sections 190.011(5), 190.011(15) and 190.035, Florida Statutes. The specific laws implemented in the Proposed Rule include, but are not limited to, Sections 112.08, 112.3143, 112.31446, 112.3145, 119.07, 119.0701, 120.54, 120.542, 120.5435, 120.56, 120.69, 120.81, 189.053, 189.069, 190.006, 190.007, 190.008, 190.011, 190.033, 190.035, 218.33, 218.391, 255.05, 255.0518, 255.0525, 255.20, 286.0105, 286.011, 286.012, 286.0113, 286.0114, 287.017, 287.055, and 287.084, Florida Statutes.
A statement of estimated regulatory costs, as defined in Section 120.541(2), Florida Statutes, has not been prepared relative to the Proposed Rule. Any person who wishes to provide the District with a proposal for a lower cost regulatory alternative as provided by Section 120.541(1), Florida Statutes, must do so in writing within twenty one (21) days after publication of this notice to the District Managers Office (as defined herein).
For more information regarding the public hearing, the Proposed Rule, or for a copy of the Proposed Rule and the related incorporated documents, if any, please contact the District Manager c/o Wrathell, Hunt & Associates, LLC, 2300 Glades Road, Suite 410W, Boca Raton, Florida 33431 Ph: 561-571-0010, torrese@whhassociates.com (the District Managers Office).
This public hearing may be continued to a date, time, and place to be specified on the record at the hearing without additional notice. If anyone chooses to appeal any decision of the Board with respect to any matter considered at the public hearing, such person will need a record of the proceedings and should accordingly ensure that a verbatim record of the proceedings is made which includes the testimony and evidence upon which such appeal is to be based. At the public hearing, staff or Supervisors may participate in the public hearing by speaker telephone.
Pursuant to the provisions of the Americans with Disabilities Act, any person requiring special accommodations at this public hearing because of a disability or physical impairment should contact the District Managers Office at least forty-eight (48) hours prior to the public hearing. If you are hearing or speech impaired, please contact the Florida Relay Service at 1-800-955-8771 or 1 800-955-8770 for aid in contacting the District Managers Office.
Ernesto Torres, District Manager South Lake Toho Community Development District
February 26, 2026

item.title: Miscellaneous Notices - Kissimmee
item.description:
HARMONY COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT DISTRICT NOTICE OF PUBLIC MEETING
Notice is hereby given that the Board of Supervisors ( Board of the Harmony Commu...
--- Full Notice Text --- HARMONY COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT DISTRICT NOTICE OF PUBLIC MEETING
Notice is hereby given that the Board of Supervisors ( Board of the Harmony Community Development District ( District will hold a regular meeting of the Board of Supervisors on February 26, 2026, at 6:00 p.m. , at the Su Mesa Caf at 7250 Harmony Sq Dr S, Harmony, FL 34773. A copy of the agenda for the meeting can be obtained from the District Office at PFM Group Consulting LLC, 3501 Quadrangle Blvd., Suite 270, Orlando, FL 32817 or by phone at (407) 723-5900. Additionally, a copy of the agenda, along with any meeting materials available in an electronic format, may be obtained at https://harmonycdd.org/
The meeting is open to the public and will be conducted in accordance with the provisions of Florida law for community development districts. The meeting may be continued to a date, time, and place to be specified on the record at such meeting. There may be occasions when Board Supervisors or District Staff may participate by speaker telephone.
Any person requiring special accommodations at the meeting because of a disability or physical impairment should contact the District Managers Office at least forty-eight (48) hours prior to the meeting. If you are hearing or speech impaired, please contact the Florida Relay Service by dialing 7-1-1, or 1-800-955-8771 (TTY) / 1-800-955-8770 (Voice), for aid in contacting the District Managers Office.
Each person who decides to appeal any decision made by the Board with respect to any matter considered at the meeting is advised that person will need a record of proceedings and that accordingly, the person may need to ensure that a verbatim record of the proceedings is made, including the testimony and evidence upon which such appeal is to be based.
Lynne Mullins District Manager February 19, 2026

item.title: Miscellaneous Notices - Orlando
item.description:
NOTICE OF CONSIDERATION OF BUDGET AMENDMENT FOR GREATER ORLANDO AVIATION AUTHORITY Notice is hereby given pursuant to Orlando City Charter Chapter ...
--- Full Notice Text --- NOTICE OF CONSIDERATION OF BUDGET AMENDMENT FOR GREATER ORLANDO AVIATION AUTHORITY Notice is hereby given pursuant to Orlando City Charter Chapter 16, Section 12, that on February 18, 2026, at 2:00 p.m., or as soon thereafter as practicable, the Greater Orlando Aviation Authority Board will consider an amendment to its budget for Fiscal Year 2026 in the Aviation Authority Board room (Carl T. Langford Board Room) at One Jeff Fuqua Boulevard, Orlando, Florida 32827-4399. A memorandum describing the budget amendment may be inspected on the Aviation Authoritys website following the posting of the agenda for the February 18, 2026, Board meeting. 24784 2/6/2026

item.title: Miscellaneous Notices - Orlando
item.description:
CITY OF EDGEWOOD NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING SECOND AND FINAL READING OF ORDINANCE On Tuesday, February 17, 2026, at 6:30 pm or as soon thereafter as...
--- Full Notice Text --- CITY OF EDGEWOOD NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING SECOND AND FINAL READING OF ORDINANCE On Tuesday, February 17, 2026, at 6:30 pm or as soon thereafter as the matter may be heard, the Edgewood City Council will hold a Public Hearing in the City Hall Council Chamber, 405 Bagshaw Way, Edgewood, FL 32809, on the following ordinance: ORDINANCE NO 2026-01 AN ORDINANCE OF THE CITY OF EDGEWOOD, FLORIDA, AMENDING CHAPTER 134 OF THE CODE OF ORDINANCES TO CORRECT A SCRIVENERS ERROR RELATED TO TATTOO ESTABLISHMENTS AND BODY PIERCING; PROVIDING FOR SEVERABILITY; PROVIDING FOR CODIFICATION, CONFLICTS, AND AN EFFECTIVE DATE. Ordinances may be inspected by the public in the Office of the City Clerk, 405 Bagshaw Way, Edgewood, FL. 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. Monday - Thursday and 8 a.m. to 12 noon on Friday or send a request for an email copy to sriffle@edgewood-fl.gov. Interested parties may appear and be heard with respect to the Ordinance. Persons wishing to appeal any decision made during the hearing will need a record of the proceedings and may need to ensure that a verbatim record is made, including the testimony and evidence on which the appeal is to be based. Persons with disabilities needing assistance or an interpreter to participate in this public hearing should contact Sandra Riffle, City Clerk, at (407) 8512920, at least 24 hours in advance of the meeting. 21811 February 5, 2026

item.title: Miscellaneous Notices - Kissimmee
item.description:
NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING ORDINANCE NO
--- Full Notice Text --- NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING ORDINANCE NO. 2026-01
Notice is hereby given that the Planning Commission of the City of St. Cloud, Florida, at a meeting to be held on February 17, 2026, in the Council Chambers at City Hall, 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, Florida at 6:00 p.m., will consider the issue and make a recommendation to the City Council relative to enactment of Ordinance No. 2026-01 entitled:
Notice is hereby given that the City Council of the City of St. Cloud, Florida, at a meeting to be held on March 12, 2026, in their Chamber at City Hall, 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, Florida at 6:30 p.m., will consider the adoption of Ordinance No. 2026-01 entitled:
AN ORDINANCE OF THE CITY COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF ST. CLOUD, FLORIDA, ASSIGNING A FUTURE LAND USE DESIGNATION OF APPROXIMATELY +/- 20.39 ACRES IDENTIFIED AS NEOCITY SOUTH PHASE 1, PROJECT #CPA25-00018, GENERALLY LOCATED NORTH OF THE FUTURE NOLTE ROAD TURNPIKE INTERCHANGE, SOUTHEAST OF CROSS PRAIRIE PARKWAY AND WEST OF THE FLORIDAS TURNPIKE, FROM MDR MEDIUM DENSITY RESIDENTIAL TO IND INDUSTRIAL, PROVIDING FOR AMENDING THE OFFICIAL FUTURE LAND USE MAP OF THE COMPREHENSIVE PLAN, FILING OF THE PLANNING COMMISSION RECOMMENDATION AND PROOF OF PUBLICATION, APPLICABILITY AND EFFECT, SEVERABILITY, COPIES ON FILE AND EFFECTIVE DATE.
Interested parties may appear at the meeting at which time this ORDINANCE is to be considered and shall be heard with respect to the proposed ORDINANCE at the Public Hearing thereon. This ORDINANCE, in its entirety can be reviewed at the Community Development Department between the hours of 8:00 a.m. and 4:00 p.m. Monday through Friday.
If a person decides to appeal any decision made by the Committee/Board, with respect to any matter considered at such hearing/meeting, such person will need a record of the proceedings and that, for this purpose, such person may need to ensure that a verbatim record of the proceedings is made, which record includes the testimony and evidence upon which the appeal is to be based, and which record is not provided by the City of St. Cloud. (FS 286.0105)
In accordance with the Americans with Disabilities Act, persons needing assistance to participate in any of these proceedings should contact the Secretary/Clerk of the Committee/Board (listed below), prior to the meeting. (FS 286.26)
Christy Depuy, Planning Commission Secretary 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, FL 34769 Telephone: (407) 957-7297
Ivy Llauro, City Clerk 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, Florida 34769 Telephone: 407-957-7300 February 5, 2026

item.title: Miscellaneous Notices - Kissimmee
item.description:
NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING ORDINANCE NO
--- Full Notice Text --- NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING ORDINANCE NO. 2026-04
Notice is hereby given that the Planning Commission of the City of St. Cloud, Florida, at a meeting to be held on February 17, 2026, in the Council Chambers at City Hall, 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, Florida at 6:00 p.m., will consider the issue and make a recommendation to the City Council relative to enactment of Ordinance No. 2026-04 entitled:
Notice is hereby given that the City Council of the City of St. Cloud, Florida, at a meeting to be held on March 12, 2026, in their Chamber at City Hall, 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, Florida at 6:30 p.m., will consider the adoption of Ordinance No. 2026-04 entitled:
AN ORDINANCE OF THE CITY COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF ST. CLOUD, FLORIDA, ASSIGNING A FUTURE LAND USE DESIGNATION OF APPROXIMATELY +/- 5.26 ACRES IDENTIFIED AS BUR OAK BOULEVARD, PROJECT #CPA25-00013, GENERALLY LOCATED NORTH OF BUR OAK BOULEVARD, EAST OF TAY WES DRIVE, SOUTH OF EAST IRLO BRONSON MEMORIAL HIGHWAY, AND WEST OF LIVE OAK LAKE ROAD, FROM COM COMMERCIAL (COUNTY) TO COM COMMERCIAL (CITY), PROVIDING FOR AMENDING THE OFFICIAL FUTURE LAND USE MAP OF THE COMPREHENSIVE PLAN, FILING OF THE PLANNING COMMISSION RECOMMENDATION AND PROOF OF PUBLICATION, APPLICABILITY AND EFFECT, SEVERABILITY, COPIES ON FILE AND EFFECTIVE DATE.
Interested parties may appear at the meeting at which time this ORDINANCE is to be considered and shall be heard with respect to the proposed ORDINANCE at the Public Hearing thereon. This ORDINANCE, in its entirety can be reviewed at the Community Development Department between the hours of 8:00 a.m. and 4:00 p.m. Monday through Friday.
If a person decides to appeal any decision made by the Committee/Board, with respect to any matter considered at such hearing/meeting, such person will need a record of the proceedings and that, for this purpose, such person may need to ensure that a verbatim record of the proceedings is made, which record includes the testimony and evidence upon which the appeal is to be based, and which record is not provided by the City of St. Cloud. (FS 286.0105)
In accordance with the Americans with Disabilities Act, persons needing assistance to participate in any of these proceedings should contact the Secretary/Clerk of the Committee/Board (listed below), prior to the meeting. (FS 286.26)
Christy Depuy, Planning Commission Secretary 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, FL 34769 Telephone: (407) 957-7297
Ivy Llauro, City Clerk 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, Florida 34769 Telephone: 407-957-7300 February 5, 2026

item.title: Miscellaneous Notices - Kissimmee
item.description:
EDGEWATER WEST COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT DISTRICT NOTICE OF MEETING
Notice is hereby given that the Board of Supervisors (Board) of the Edgewater Wes...
--- Full Notice Text --- EDGEWATER WEST COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT DISTRICT NOTICE OF MEETING
Notice is hereby given that the Board of Supervisors (Board) of the Edgewater West Community Development District (District), will hold a Special Meeting on February 18, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., or as soon thereafter as the matter may be heard, at the offices of Hanson, Walter & Associates, Inc., 8 Broadway, Suite 104, Kissimmee, Florida 34741.
The purpose of the meeting is for the Board to consider updated financing plans for the next bond issuance, as well as any business that may be lawfully considered. The meeting is open to the public and will be conducted in accordance with the provision of Florida Law for Community Development Districts. The meeting may be continued to a date, time, and place to be specified on the record at the meeting. A copy of the agenda for the meeting may be obtained from the office of the District Manager, Wrathell, Hunt & Associates, LLC, 2300 Glades Road, Suite 410W, Boca Raton, Florida 33431, (877) 276-0889, during normal business hours or by visiting the districts website, https://edgewaterwestcdd.net/.
There may be occasions when one or more Supervisors or staff will participate by telephone. Pursuant to provisions of the Americans with Disabilities Act, any person requiring special accommodations at this meeting because of a disability or physical impairment should contact the District Office at (877) 276-0889 at least 48 hours prior to the meeting. If you are hearing or speech impaired, please contact the Florida Relay Service by dialing 7-1-1, or 1-800-955-8771 (TTY) / 1-800-955-8770 (Voice), for aid in contacting the District Office.
A person who decides to appeal any decision made at the meeting with respect to any matter considered at the meeting is advised that person will need a record of the proceedings and that accordingly, the person may need to ensure that a verbatim record of the proceedings is made, including the testimony and evidence upon which such appeal is to be based.
District Manager February 5, 2026

item.title: Miscellaneous Notices - Kissimmee
item.description:
NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING ORDINANCE NO
--- Full Notice Text --- NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING ORDINANCE NO. 2026-05
Notice is hereby given that the Planning Commission of the City of St. Cloud, Florida, at a meeting to be held on February 17, 2026, in the Council Chambers at City Hall, 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, Florida at 6:00 p.m., will consider the issue and make a recommendation to the City Council relative to enactment of Ordinance No. 2026-05 entitled:
Notice is hereby given that the City Council of the City of St. Cloud, Florida, at a meeting to be held on March 12, 2026, in their Chamber at City Hall, 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, Florida at 6:30 p.m., will consider the adoption of Ordinance No. 2026-04 entitled:
AN ORDINANCE OF THE CITY COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF ST. CLOUD, FLORIDA, PROVIDING FOR THE ZONING AMENDMENT OF THE ZONING MAP OF THE LAND DEVELOPMENT CODE OF THE CITY OF ST. CLOUD, FLORIDA, SPECIFICALLY CHANGING THE ZONING DESIGNATION OF APPROXIMATELY +/- 5.26 ACRES IDENTIFIED AS BUR OAK BOULEVARD, PROJECT #PUD26-00001 FORMERLY ZMA25-00017, FROM PD PLANNED DEVELOPMENT (COUNTY) TO A PUD PLANNED UNIT DEVELOPMENT / PRELIMINARY MASTER PLAN (PUD/PMP ) (CITY) ZONING DESIGNATION, WITH A COMMERCIAL FUTURE LAND USE DESIGNATION ADOPTED BY ORDINANCE NO. 2026-04, GENERALLY LOCATED NORTH OF BUR OAK BOULEVARD, EAST OF TAY WES DRIVE, SOUTH OF EAST IRLO BRONSON MEMORIAL HIGHWAY, AND WEST OF LIVE OAK LAKE ROAD, PROVIDING FOR ENTERING THE DESIGNATION ON THE OFFICIAL ZONING MAP; FILING OF THE PLANNING COMMISSION RECOMMENDATION AND PROOF OF PUBLICATION, SEVERABILITY, AND EFFECTIVE DATE.
Interested parties may appear at the meeting at which time this ORDINANCE is to be considered and shall be heard with respect to the proposed ORDINANCE at the Public Hearing thereon. This ORDINANCE, in its entirety can be reviewed at the Community Development Department between the hours of 8:00 a.m. and 4:00 p.m. Monday through Friday.
If a person decides to appeal any decision made by the Committee/Board, with respect to any matter considered at such hearing/meeting, such person will need a record of the proceedings and that, for this purpose, such person may need to ensure that a verbatim record of the proceedings is made, which record includes the testimony and evidence upon which the appeal is to be based, and which record is not provided by the City of St. Cloud. (FS 286.0105)
In accordance with the Americans with Disabilities Act, persons needing assistance to participate in any of these proceedings should contact the Secretary/Clerk of the Committee/Board (listed below), prior to the meeting. (FS 286.26)
Christy Depuy, Planning Commission Secretary 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, FL 34769 Telephone: (407) 957-7297
Ivy Llauro, City Clerk 1300 9th Street, St. Cloud, Florida 34769 Telephone: 407-957-7300 February 5, 2026

item.title: Miscellaneous Notices - Orlando
item.description:
PLANNING AND ZONING COMMISSION / LOCAL PLANNING AGENCY PUBLIC HEARING AGENDA February 19, 2026 DEAR PROPERTY OWNER OR INTERESTED PARTY: At 9:00 a
--- Full Notice Text --- PLANNING AND ZONING COMMISSION / LOCAL PLANNING AGENCY PUBLIC HEARING AGENDA February 19, 2026 DEAR PROPERTY OWNER OR INTERESTED PARTY: At 9:00 a.m. on Thursday, February 19, 2026, or as soon thereafter as the matters may be considered, the Orange County Planning and Zoning Commission (PZC) / Local Planning Agency (LPA) shall hold a public hearing to consider the rezoning requests listed below and public hearing for an ordinance as described below. Also, at 9:00 a.m. on Thursday, February 19, 2026, or as soon thereafter as the matters may be considered, the Orange County PZC / Local Planning Agency (LPA) shall hold public hearings to consider the adoption of Small-Scale Development Amendments to the Orange County Comprehensive Plan (CP), as authorized by Chapter 163, Florida Statutes and pursuant to Sections 163.3187(2) and 125.66, Florida Statutes. PZC / LPA public hearings are held in the Board of County Commissioners Chambers located on the 1st Floor of the Orange County Administration Building, 201 South Rosalind Avenue, Orlando, Florida. The staff report book and meeting agenda will also be available for public viewing on the Orange County website (www.orangecountyfl.net) one week prior to the public hearing. Please be advised that this notice is published in accordance with the Orange County Code, Section 30-1(d)(6) and Section 30-40 as well as the provisions of the Orange County Comprehensive Plan and applicable state law. Section 286.0105, Florida Statutes, states that if a person decides to appeal any decision made by a board, agency, or commission with respect to any matter considered at a meeting or hearing, he or she will need a record of the proceedings, and that, for such purpose, he or she may need to ensure that a verbatim record of the proceedings is made, which record includes the testimony and evidence upon which the appeal is to be based. In accordance with the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA), if any person with a disability as defined by the ADA needs special accommodations to participate in this proceeding, then not later than two (2) business days prior to the proceedings, he or she should contact the Government Service Center at (407) 836-3111. Alberto A. Vargas Planning Manager Para mas informacion referente a esta vista publica, favor comunicarse al Departamento de Planificacion al numero 407-836-5600. Pou plis enfomasyon an Kreyol souple rele (407)836-3111. AGENDA: 9:00 A.M. Pledge of Allegiance, review of the January 15, 2026, PZC/LPA meeting minutes, and public comment. The following public hearings will occur after public comment: Privately Initiated Rezoning Request: RZ-26-02-042 APPLICANT: Arthur Stagg OWNER: Arthur Stagg REQUEST: To rezone from R-1A (Single-Family Dwelling District) to R-1 (Single-Family Dwelling District) LOCATION: 1111 Timor Ave; generally located north of W. Fairbanks Ave, west of Adanson Street, east of Fairview Ave, and south of Carlson Drive LEGAL: Legal description on file in the Planning Division TRACT SIZE: 0.15 gross acres DISTRICT: 5 RZ-26-02-044 APPLICANT: Aldo Maldonado OWNER: Gold Millie Estate REQUEST: To rezone from A-2 (Farmland Rural District) to R-2 (Residential District) LOCATION: 7409 and 7429 Restful Street; generally located north of Restful Street, south of University Boulevard, east of Wilde Avenue, and west of Goldenrod Road LEGAL: Legal description on file in the Planning Division TRACT SIZE: 1.96 gross acres DISTRICT: 5 RZ-26-02-046 APPLICANT: Mohammad Rahman OWNER: Rahman Mohammad Sohel REQUEST: To rezone from A-2 (Farmland Rural District) to I-1/I-5 (Industrial District-Light) LOCATION: 7152 Memory Lane; generally located south of Memory Lane, east of N. Forsyth Road, west of Greshan Drive, and north of Partridge Lane LEGAL: Legal description on file in the Planning Division TRACT SIZE: 0.38 gross acre DISTRICT: 5 RZ-26-01-034 APPLICANT: Duc Hien Nguyen OWNER: Duc Hien Nguyen REQUEST: To rezone from A-2 (Farmland Rural District) to R-2 (Residential District) LOCATION: 8206, 8224, and 8236 Curry Ford Road; generally located south of Curry Ford Road, east of Autumn Run Place, and west of Los Amigos Drive. LEGAL: Legal description on file in the Planning Division TRACT SIZE: 3.82 gross acres DISTRICT: 3 RZ-26-01-038 APPLICANT: Ho Ho Van OWNER: Nghia Nguyen REQUEST: To rezone from R-T-2 (Combination Mobile Home and Single-Family Dwelling District) to R-T-1 (Mobile Home Subdivision District) LOCATION: 526 and 528 Shepard Road; generally north of Mercury Road, east of Story Partin Road, west of S. County Road 13, and south of E. Colonial Drive LEGAL: Legal description on file in the Planning Division TRACT SIZE: 0.62 gross acre DISTRICT: 5 Privately Initiated Small-Scale Future Land Use Element Map Amendments to Change the Designation of Lands within Unincorporated County, and Where Expressly Noted Below, Concurrent Rezoning Requests SS-26-02-039 APPLICANT: Lonnie Mack Caldwell, Caldwell Design Build, LLC OWNER: KHJ Investments, LLC REQUEST: To change the Future Land Use Map designation from Low Density Residential (LDR) to Low-Medium Density Residential (LMDR) LOCATION: 6606 Winegard Road; generally located on the west side of Winegard Road, south of W. Lancaster Road, north of Orwell Avenue, and east of Beret Road LEGAL: Legal description on file in the Planning Division TRACT SIZE: 0.83 gross acre DISTRICT: 3 Public Hearing AN ORDINANCE PERTAINING TO THE BUILDING AND CONSTRUCTION REGULATIONS OF ORANGE COUNTY, FLORIDA; AMENDING CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF CHAPTER 9 (BUILDING AND CONSTRUCTION REGULATIONS) OF THE ORANGE COUNTY CODE BY UPDATING SECTION 9-4 (BUILDING CODES BOARD OF ADJUSTMENTS AND APPEALS (BCBAA); CREATION; MEMBERSHIP; MEETINGS; QUORUM; POWERS AND DUTIES); AMENDING ARTICLE II (BUILDING CODE); BY REVISING SECTION 9-33 (FLORIDA BUILDING CODE, BUILDING, ADOPTED) AND SECTION 9-39 (FLORIDA BUILDING CODE, EXISTING BUILDING, ADOPTED) FOR CONSISTENCY; AND PROVIDING AN EFFECTIVE DATE. 22673 2/4/2026

item.title: Miscellaneous Notices - Orlando
item.description:
Notice of Public Meeting Dates of the Construction Committee of the Sunbridge Stewardship District Tuesday, February 10, 2026, at 1:30 p
--- Full Notice Text --- Notice of Public Meeting Dates of the Construction Committee of the Sunbridge Stewardship District Tuesday, February 10, 2026, at 1:30 p.m.The Construction Committee of the Sunbridge Stewardship District will be meeting on Tuesday, February 10, 2026, at 1:30 p.m. at 6900 Tavistock Lakes Blvd, Ste 200, Orlando, FL 32827. The Construction Committee will discuss and review the status of current and future Infrastructure Improvements, Requests for Qualifications, Requests for Proposals, etc., related to the District. The meeting will be open to the public and will be conducted in accordance with provisions of Florida Law for Community Development Districts. The meetings may be continued to a date, time, and place to be specified on the record at the meeting. There may be occasions when one or more members of the Committee and/or District Staff will participate by telephone. Pursuant to the provisions of the Americans with Disabilities Act, any person requiring special accommodations at these meetings because of a disability or physical impairment should contact the District Managers Office at 407-723-5900 at least forty-eight (48) hours prior to the meeting. If you are hearing or speech impaired, please contact the Florida Relay Service by dialing 7-1-1, or 1-800-955-8771 (TTY) / 1-800-955-8770 (Voice), for aid in contacting the District Managers Office. Any person who decides to appeal any decision made by the Committee with respect to any matter considered at the meeting is advised that the person will need a record of the proceedings and that, accordingly, the person may need to ensure that a verbatim record of the proceedings is made, including the testimony and evidence upon which such appeal is to be based. Lynne Mullins District Manager 7916159 2/3/2026

item.title: Miscellaneous Notices - Orlando
item.description:
Notice of Public Meeting Dates of the Construction Committee of the Sunbridge Stewardship District The Construction Committee of the Sunbridge Stew...
--- Full Notice Text --- Notice of Public Meeting Dates of the Construction Committee of the Sunbridge Stewardship District The Construction Committee of the Sunbridge Stewardship District will be meeting on Tuesday, February 10, 2026, at 1:30 p.m. at 6900 Tavistock Lakes Blvd, Ste 200, Orlando, FL 32827. The Construction Committee will discuss and review the status of current and future Infrastructure Improvements, Requests for Qualifications, Requests for Proposals, etc., related to the District. The meeting will be open to the public and will be conducted in accordance with provisions of Florida Law for Community Development Districts. The meetings may be continued to a date, time, and place to be specified on the record at the meeting. There may be occasions when one or more members of the Committee and/or District Staff will participate by telephone. Pursuant to the provisions of the Americans with Disabilities Act, any person requiring special accommodations at these meetings because of a disability or physical impairment should contact the District Managers Office at 407-723-5900 at least forty-eight (48) hours prior to the meeting. If you are hearing or speech impaired, please contact the Florida Relay Service by dialing 7-1-1, or 1-800-955-8771 (TTY) / 1-800-955-8770 (Voice), for aid in contacting the District Managers Office. Any person who decides to appeal any decision made by the Committee with respect to any matter considered at the meeting is advised that the person will need a record of the proceedings and that, accordingly, the person may need to ensure that a verbatim record of the proceedings is made, including the testimony and evidence upon which such appeal is to be based. Lynne Mullins District Manager 19591 2/3/2026

item.title: Miscellaneous Notices - Orlando
item.description:
MEETING NOTICE MetroPlan Orlando, The Metropolitan Planning Organization for the Orlando Urban Area, announces the following public meeting of the ...
--- Full Notice Text --- MEETING NOTICE MetroPlan Orlando, The Metropolitan Planning Organization for the Orlando Urban Area, announces the following public meeting of the Transportation Disadvantaged Local Coordinating Board (TDLCB), to which all persons are invited: Date: Thursday, February 12, 2026 Time: 10:00 a.m. Place: MetroPlan Orlando 250 S. Orange Avenue, Suite 200 Orlando, FL 32801 Purpose: Regularly Scheduled TDLCB Meeting A copy of the detailed agendas for these meetings may be obtained by contacting Ms. Rachel Frederick, Board Services Coordinator, MetroPlan Orlando, 250 S. Orange Avenue, Suite 200, Orlando, FL 32801, 407-481-5672, E-mail: Rachel.Frederick@MetroPlanOrlando.gov Public participation is conducted without regard to race, color, national origin, sex, age, disability, religion, or family status. Persons wishing to express concerns, who require special assistance under the Americans with Disabilities Act, or who require language services (free of charge) should contact MetroPlan Orlando by phone at (407) 481-5672 or by email at info@metroplanorlando.gov at least three business days prior to the event. Anuncio de Reunin MetroPlan Orlando la organizacin de planificacin metropolitana (MPO) de los condados Orange, Osceola y Seminole anuncia la siguiente hbrido reunin pblica de la Junta de Coordinacin Local para Personas con Desventaja de Transporte (TDLCB), la cual todos estn invitados: Dia: el jueves, 12 de febrero del 2026 Hora: 10:00 a.m. Lugar: MetroPlan Orlando 250 S. Orange Avenue, Suite 200 Orlando, FL 32801 Propsito: Reunin Regularmente Programada Para obtener copias de la agenda de esta reunin por favor contacten a la Sra. Rachel Frederick, asistente administrativa, MetroPlan Orlando, 250 S. Orange Avenue, Suite 200, Orlando, FL 32801, 407-481-5672, email: Rachel.Frederick@MetroPlanOrlando.gov La participacin pblica se lleva a cabo sin distincin de raza, color, origen nacional, sexo, edad, discapacidad, religin o estado familiar. Las personas que deseen expresar inquietudes, que requieran asistencia especial bajo la Ley de Americanos con Discapacidad (ADA) o que requieran servicios de traduccin (sin cargo) deben ponerse en contacto con MetroPlan Orlando por telfono (407) 481-5672 (marcar 0) o por correo electrnico info@metroplanorlando.gov por lo menos tres das antes del evento. Publish Date: Orlando Sentinel Legal Notices Monday, February 2, 2026 21766 2/2/26

item.title: Miscellaneous Notices - Orlando
item.description:
NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING COMPREHENSIVE DEVELOPMENT PLAN (CDP) TEXT AMENDMENT CITY OF MAITLAND, FLORIDA The City Council of the City of Maitland, Fl...
Meeting: Monday, February 23, 2026 at 6:30 P.M.
--- Full Notice Text --- NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING COMPREHENSIVE DEVELOPMENT PLAN (CDP) TEXT AMENDMENT CITY OF MAITLAND, FLORIDA The City Council of the City of Maitland, Florida, proposes to adopt an ordinance to amend the CDP. The proposed CDP amendment ordinance #1446 is entitled: AN ORDINANCE OF THE CITY OF MAITLAND, FLORIDA, AMENDING THE CDP - FUTURE LAND USE ELEMENT, CORRIDOR DISTRICT, AREA OF SPECIAL CONSIDERATION MAITLAND CONCOURSE NORTH, TABLE 1.1.3.11, MAITLAND CONCOURSE NORTH, PROPERTY OVERALL LAND USE MIX, LAND USE TYPE, AMENDING THE RESIDENTIAL USE TYPE TO CATEGORIZE EXISTING 350 UNITS AS MULTI-FAMILY UNITS AND TO ADD 85 TOWNHOMES TO THE RESIDENTIAL USE TYPE AND EXCLUDE RESIDENTIAL PARKING GARAGES FROM FLOOR AREA RATIO CALCULATIONS AND AMENDING STANDARD 1.1.3.10 TO EXCLUDE RESIDENTIAL PARKING GARAGES FROM FLOOR AREA RATIO CALCULATIONS, AND PROVIDING FOR AN EFFECTIVE DATE. A public hearing on the ordinance will be held by the City Council of the City of Maitland, Florida, on Monday, February 23, 2026 at 6:30 P.M., or as soon thereafter as is reasonably possible in the Maitland City Hall Council Chambers, 1776 Independence Lane, Maitland, Florida 32751. The purpose of the public hearing is to take public comment, both written and oral, and to consider adoption of the ordinance to amend the City of Maitland (CDP), in accordance with F.S. 163, Part II. A proposed amendment to the City of Maitlands Comprehensive Development Plan has been submitted by Kimley-Horn and Associates, Inc., 200 S. Orange Avenue., Suite 600, Orlando, Florida 32801. The property owner is BPL Maitland Concourse North, LLC., P.O. Box 3010, Winter Park, Florida 32790. The applicant requests a text amendment to the CDP - Future Land Use Element, Corridor District, Area of Special Consideration Maitland Concourse North, Table 1.1.3.11, Maitland Concourse North, Property Overall Land Use Mix, Land Use Type to recognize the existing 350 units as multi-family and to add 85 townhomes to the residential use type; and to exclude residential parking garages from floor area ratio calculations. A complete copy of the ordinance and proposed CDP Amendment may be inspected by any interested person, Monday-Thursday, 7:00 A.M.- 6:00 P.M., in the Community Development Department, 1776 Independence Lane, Maitland, Florida 32751. Interested parties may attend the public hearing to be heard by the City Council or may submit written comments at or before the public hearing to the attention of the Community Development Department of the City of Maitland, Florida. Questions concerning the notice of public hearing may be directed to the Community Development Department at (407) 539-6212. The hearing may be continued from time to time. Please be advised that in accordance with State Law, any person who desires to appeal any decision made at these meetings or hearings will need a record of the proceedings and, for this purpose, may need to ensure that a verbatim record of the proceedings is made which includes testimony and evidence upon which the appeal is to be based. Persons with disability needing assistance to participate in any of these proceedings should contact the City Clerk's office (407) 539-6219 48 hours in advance of the meeting. Sara Blanchard Chief Planner City of Maitland 21429 2/2/26
